And so there's only so and everybody you've only got so much you may have more than me You may have less than me, but you do only have so much and you can only stretch it so thin Before you've used it all up. So why put yourself in? those environments so Like I I When I quit when I quit drinking I stayed out of bars and didn't have any booze in the house for a couple of years And it turns out it doesn't matter to me I could sit at a bar all night with everybody drinking and drink iced tea and wind up the last sober one in the room and It has no impact on me what whatsoever But that's not necessarily true of somebody else who's had a serious drinking problem But and they keep putting themselves back in that environment and it's ridiculous You know because it's good to get them and after the second or third time it's got them They should get the message you've got to fix your environment So if you want extreme productivity environment is a real issue control of other people is a real issue and and there if you're your worst enemy everybody else pretty much as your second worst enemy Because See they don't get it and in their defense You really can't expect them to have gotten it because they've never been around somebody either making or trying to make seven figures And they probably never will be Again, right? So why would we expect them to know how to behave? Around such a person I Mean you see you can't blame. I just client We did infomer an infomercial for them. They're a very big company that sells This is a This is a company that sells weekend vacation homes time shares up in the the wilds of Pennsylvania To mostly to two constituencies from Manhattan Okay, sort of elderly well-to-do Jewish people and ironically Massifluent blacks Okay, and so those are the two constituencies Most of them have like like never been into the wilds of Pennsylvania. They've been in Manhattan So we're running this TV show in Manhattan And they come up and they stay for the weekend and in a nice cabin and then they buy their timeshare on it Problem places full of bears And and they ain't never seen bears and when so when when Herbie and Golda come out of the cabin in the morning to pick up their newspaper Right, which got to have newspapers there because they're used to get newspapers So there's somebody that has to run around in the woods in the morning to all the cabins and Leave a copy of the New York Times or you're not going to sell any time shares They figured that out pretty quick So when they come out with a little coffee to get their newspaper if there's a big black bear and two smaller baby bears laying on the New York Times on The porch and when they open the door the bear gets up and goes This sort of ruins the sale Um They're all on the phone Screeching come get me there's a big and in some cases they don't even know it's a bear. There's this big giant black thing you know some of the Golda's think it's the affluent black guy and That's as big that messes up to say all as bad as the bear but you can't you but some of them feed them Okay, so That was the bigger problem because they actually had people getting like Assaulted and eaten because so they opened the door and the bears out there laying on the Times and they actually go back inside And they get a little bagel on it And they come out because they think it's like some big dog You know and they and they give the bagel to the bear and in case you don't know see the bear will very politely eat the bagel It's just that when you don't have any more bagel he eats you That was sort of the appetizer and It just kind of got him going you know like if you have a dog I don't know like our dog if you give him a little piece of people food He then immediately runs over and eats his dog food afterwards the people food was just an appetizer that got him stimulated And now he goes and eat anything else that didn't nail down Well the bear is in a sense a great big dog except that
